summary: Quick Coach feature fully verified end-to-end at 390px mobile viewport. Backend: 6/6 pytest passing — cache hit returns instantly (<5s) with valid 4-section schema, two cached calls return identical generated_at, force=true triggers a real GPT call (~10s) that updates generated_at and content, 404 for unknown project, 400 when no character selected, second call after force returns the new cached value. Frontend: all 11 UI scenarios passing on Demo Script (SARAH) — FAB visible bottom-20 right-4 (clear of Emergent badge), panel slides up with cached content instantly, all 4 sections render (Casting Intent, How to Play It, What to Avoid, Takes), 3 distinct takes with label+direction, backdrop present, X close dismisses, FAB returns, reopen still cached, FAB & panel work in Rehearse mode, Regenerate button shows loading state then updates content with new GPT call (toast 'Coaching regenerated.' confirmed). No regressions found.
action_items: (Carried over from iteration_32) rehearse-skip-btn @ 390px is occluded by the Emergent badge in PrepView rehearse mode — not in scope of this run.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1796 — coach_cache check uses truthiness on the stored dict; if a partial empty dict ever got cached this would still skip GPT. Safe today since we only set the cache after a successful GPT response, but consider gating on a presence-of-required-fields check.

server.py:1862 — broad `except Exception as e` re-raises a 500 with the raw exception text in detail. Acceptable for an internal MVP, but the verbatim error string can leak provider/SDK internals to the client. Consider mapping to a generic 'Coaching service unavailable' for production.

PrepView.jsx:162 — POST /quick-coach without an explicit timeout; if the backend hangs the user has no recovery beyond closing the panel. The call is inside an awaited try/catch which will eventually surface a network error, so non-blocking but not ideal.
PrepView.jsx:166 — On error the panel is force-closed and a toast shown; the user loses any prior cached coach_data display state. Minor UX nit — cached data could be preserved on transient errors.
PrepView.jsx:269 — `coachData.takes && takes.length > 0` is correct, but no max cap; if GPT returns >3 takes (prompt allows up to 3 but model could deviate) all are rendered. Probably fine.
PrepView.jsx covers 600 lines including coach panel, read mode, rehearse mode — borderline of the 700-line guideline. Splitting CoachPanel into its own component would improve readability.
seed_data_creation: Used existing Demo Script project (id=017a5a4f-9d91-4c92-9012-47d1c854d8e9, char=SARAH, has reviewed_lines + coach_cache). Backend tests run against this project for cache & force-regenerate verification. NOTE: after this run the Demo Script coach_cache has been regenerated twice (test_force + UI Regenerate click), so the cached coaching content reflects the latest GPT output.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Quick Coach feature is FULLY VERIFIED. Backend pytest at /app/backend/tests/test_quick_coach.py covers: (1) cache hit speed + schema (<5s, all 4 fields + takes); (2) cache returns identical generated_at; (3) 404 for unknown project; (4) 400 for project without selected_character (auto-discovers a project that has none); (5) force=true triggers real GPT (~10s) and changes generated_at + content; (6) post-force normal call returns the new cached value. Frontend UI scenarios verified: FAB position bottom-20 right-4 (clear of Emergent 'Made with' badge at bottom-4), panel slide-up animation works, all 4 CoachSection items render, 3 takes with label+direction, backdrop & X close dismiss, FAB reappears, re-open uses cached state (no spinner), works from both Read AND Rehearse modes, Regenerate button shows loading spinner then updates content with success toast. No bugs filed. The coach_cache approach (project-level field) prevents redundant GPT calls correctly.
